# Test if-elif-else with inventory management system

class InventoryItem:
    name: str
    quantity: int
    reorder_level: int
    max_capacity: int

    def __init__(self, name: str, quantity: int, reorder_level: int, max_capacity: int):
        self.name = name
        self.quantity = quantity
        self.reorder_level = reorder_level
        self.max_capacity = max_capacity

    def check_status(self) -> int:
        if self.quantity == 0:
            return 0
        elif self.quantity <= self.reorder_level:
            return 1
        elif self.quantity >= self.max_capacity:
            return 2
        else:
            return 3

    def add_stock(self, amount: int) -> bool:
        new_quantity = self.quantity + amount
        if new_quantity > self.max_capacity:
            return False
        else:
            self.quantity = new_quantity
            return True

    def remove_stock(self, amount: int) -> bool:
        new_quantity = self.quantity - amount
        if new_quantity < 0:
            return False
        else:
            self.quantity = new_quantity
            return True

class WarehouseManager:
    low_stock_count: int
    empty_stock_count: int
    overstocked_count: int

    def __init__(self):
        self.low_stock_count = 0
        self.empty_stock_count = 0
        self.overstocked_count = 0

    def analyze_item(self, item: InventoryItem) -> None:
        status = item.check_status()
        if status == 0:
            self.empty_stock_count += 1
        elif status == 1:
            self.low_stock_count += 1
        elif status == 2:
            self.overstocked_count += 1

def main():
    item1 = InventoryItem("Widgets", 5, 10, 100)
    item2 = InventoryItem("Gadgets", 0, 15, 80)
    item3 = InventoryItem("Tools", 100, 20, 100)
    
    manager = WarehouseManager()
    
    manager.analyze_item(item1)
    manager.analyze_item(item2)
    manager.analyze_item(item3)
    
    print(manager.low_stock_count)
    print(manager.empty_stock_count)
    print(manager.overstocked_count)
    
    success = item1.add_stock(50)
    print(success)
    print(item1.quantity)
    
    success = item3.add_stock(10)
    print(success)
    
    success = item2.remove_stock(5)
    print(success)

main()

# EXPECTED OUTPUT:
# 1
# 1
# 1
# True
# 55
# False
# False
